questions.question: X is a crystalline salt of sodium that forms a white precipitate with BaCl2 insoluble in HCl. Identify X.
questions.explanation: Sulfates form insoluble BaSO4 with BaCl2. Na2SO4 (key E) is the sodium sulfate that fits all conditions.

questions.question: The alkanol obtained from soap production is:
questions.explanation: Soap production (saponification) yields glycerol as a by-product.

questions.question: The flame used by welders is:
questions.explanation: Oxy-acetylene flame produces very high temperature (around 3500°C), making it ideal for welding.

questions.question: Members of alkane homologous series differ by:
questions.explanation: Successive members of any homologous series differ by CH2 (methylene group).

questions.question: Electronic configuration 1s2 2s2 2p6 3s2 3p2 belongs to:
questions.explanation: The configuration ends in 3p2, placing this element (Silicon) in the p-block.

questions.question: The shape of methane molecule is:
questions.explanation: Methane (CH4) has 4 bonding pairs and no lone pairs, giving it a perfect tetrahedral geometry.

questions.question: Reaction of alcohol with ethanoic acid forms:
questions.explanation: Esterification (acid + alcohol) produces an ester and water.

questions.question: Relative molecular mass 180 with ratio 1:2:1 (C:H:O) suggests:
questions.explanation: Glucose has formula C6H12O6 with molar mass 180 g/mol and C:H:O ratio of 1:2:1.

questions.question: Fermentation is used in:
questions.explanation: Fermentation (yeast converting sugars to ethanol + CO2) is the key process in brewing.

questions.question: Mixture of NaCl, NH4Cl, BaSO4 is best separated by:
questions.explanation: NH4Cl sublimes on heating (removed first), then NaCl dissolves in water leaving BaSO4 as residue after filtration.

questions.question: The ideal gas relation is:
questions.explanation: From the ideal gas law PV=nRT, at constant n: P∝T/V.

questions.question: A gas evolved in a reaction turns blue litmus paper to:
questions.explanation: Acidic gases (e.g. HCl, SO2) turn blue litmus red.

questions.question: Calcium flame colour is:
questions.explanation: Calcium gives a characteristic brick-red flame in a flame test.

questions.question: Endothermic reaction is favored by:
questions.explanation: By Le Chatelier's principle, increasing temperature shifts equilibrium toward the endothermic direction.

questions.question: Loss of water of crystallization is:
questions.explanation: Efflorescence is the spontaneous loss of water of crystallization to the surrounding atmosphere.

questions.question: Electronegative elements tend to:
questions.explanation: Electronegativity is the tendency to attract and gain electrons in a bond.

questions.question: Stainless steel contains:
questions.explanation: Chromium (Cr) provides the corrosion resistance that makes steel 'stainless'.

questions.question: Petroleum pollution on water surfaces is best removed by:
questions.explanation: Detergents emulsify oil, breaking it into tiny droplets that can be dispersed and removed.

questions.question: Increasing pressure on a gaseous equilibrium shifts the reaction to:

options.text: side with fewer moles of gas
side with more moles of gas

questions.explanation: By Le Chatelier's principle, increased pressure favors the side with fewer moles of gas to reduce pressure.

questions.question: The valency of oxygen is:
questions.explanation: Oxygen has 6 valence electrons and needs 2 more to complete its octet, giving it a valency of 2.
